title: Chapter Thirty-Three: Blade Mountain Sword Forest
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

They rode half a day and stopped at a village inn as sunset bled across the sky.

The place was crowded. Wandering warriors carrying swords and talking loudly. Men and women in silk martial uniforms who appeared to be from noble families. Even the waiter wore an unpleasant expression -- too many blades in too small a room.

They shared a table near the entrance with some middle-aged men and exchanged bows.

Yeon-shin's ears caught the noise before his eyes found the source.

"...the Mount Hua Sect secretly received help from Desolate Sect. The Nine Sects and Desolate Sect are setting suns. Only the Zhongnan Sect that defeated the Tyrant Sword Sect can act big in Shaanxi!"

A wandering warrior, boasting at the opposite table. A young master with a dignified bearing -- silk robes, two women companions wearing swords -- raised one eyebrow.

"I am telling you this because you are new to the martial world," the young master said, "but it is a place where you live long only if you keep your mouth shut."

The wandering warrior scoffed. "I am not new! Where am I wrong? They cannot even catch one Thieving Ghost! One eye of the Blue Eyes Demon Sword -- Radiant Demon Wing's pride -- flew off! Desolate Sect's Blue-level masters? Empty fame!"

His gaze swept the room for agreement.

*Puhk.*

The chopstick split the air without a sound. It embedded itself in the wandering warrior's table with such force that the wood cracked and split from the point of impact.

Every head in the inn turned.

Yeon-shin sat calmly at the entrance, looking at the man.

"That story," he said. Sky-blue light flickered in his eyes. "Tell it in more detail."

True energy laced his low voice. No one in the room could have failed to hear.

The inn went silent.

Hyeon Won-chang reached over and turned the shoulder of Yeon-shin's blue robe forward. The white-embroidered character *Hwang* gleamed in the lamplight.

"Desolate Sect?" The young master's posture changed. Interest sharpened behind his composure.

The wandering warrior's face had turned the colour of ash. "Are you truly a Desolate Sect martial artist?"

"Our sect was insulted by your mouth," Hyeon Won-chang said, his expression harder than Yeon-shin had ever seen it. "Do not think my patience will last."

Yeon-shin kept silent. He twirled the remaining chopstick between his index and middle fingers. The wandering warrior's complexion drained further.

"I -- I had no intention to insult Desolate Sect. I just --"

"I am not interested in your apology. You said Blue Eyes Demon Sword lost an eye. Talk about that."

The words poured out like water from a broken jar. Thieving Ghost. The Blood Flame Sect behind him. Blood Swordmasters and an apostle clashing with Radiant Demon Wing. Cheong Myeong, protecting a civilian family, taking a hidden weapon to the face.

*Cheong Myeong.*

It sounded exactly like him. The man who smiled through everything and would not step aside when innocents stood behind him.

"After the Tyrant Sword Sect, the Blood Flame Sect?" Hyeon Won-chang muttered.

Yeon-shin nodded slowly. The two sects that had destroyed his family. The same two that kept appearing, again and again, like wolves circling a camp.

"You wagered your neck when you ran your mouth," Yeon-shin said to the wandering warrior.

The man's body went rigid. Then he bowed deeply. "I apologise!"

The attitude of a man staring at a messenger of death. Yeon-shin looked at him and felt, strangely, as though he were looking at a reflection. A man throwing away pride to preserve his life. His own neck was that precious.

*That is me. Trading achievements for survival. Begging the Desolate Sect Lord for one more year.*

"Just eat," he said. "Pay the innkeeper for the table."

---

The young master introduced himself as Jin Yul of the Shaanxi Jin family. His manner was warm, his insight rich, his words precisely calibrated to lift others. Everything about him was too polished for a small martial family.

He asked to accompany them to Lantian County. Hyeon Won-chang deferred to Yeon-shin.

"You can guess our destination?" Yeon-shin said. "You know the circumstances in detail?"

"After the battle between Zhongnan and the Tyrant Sword Sect, the Radiant Demon Wing and the Mount Hua faction are the hottest topic in Xi'an. Even those from small families cannot help but know."

Yeon-shin studied him.

*He was at the first inn we stopped at after leaving Zhongnan Mountain. As if he had been waiting.*

He nodded. Kept the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique simmering at low intensity in his meridians.

They rode together. Jin Yul and Hyeon Won-chang became fast friends -- Hyeon Won-chang recounted the beheading of the Rakshasa with heroic embellishment, and Jin Yul praised every word with the skill of a born courtier.

Midday. The sun high and hot. A stream beside the road.

"Let us rest," Jin Yul said.

Hyeon Won-chang volunteered to tend the horses. Yeon-shin walked to the stream and crouched to fill his flask.

Jin Yul sat close on his left.

"Sir Jeong, do you perhaps use your left hand as well?"

"I use my right for sword techniques. Some fist techniques, superficially, with the left."

"I see. Then your left side must be a weakness."

The tone was casual. Like discussing the weather.

The content registered a heartbeat late.

A dagger thrust came without energy force -- stealthy, fast, aimed at the ribs beneath his left arm. An assassin's blade.

Yeon-shin had been ready since the inn.

The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique surged. Internal energy flooded his body, heavier than before. His left arm was already moving -- Eternal Blossom Fist's key technique activating in the same instant a chilling sword light rose from the diagonal.

Hyeon Won-chang's Desolate Sword.

The flash cut through Jin Yul's neck.

Blood sprayed into the stream. The body pitched forward, face plunging into the water.

"I knew this would happen," Hyeon Won-chang said. He stood over the corpse, wiping his blade on the dead man's robes. His face was serene -- as if the friendship of the ride had never existed.

Yeon-shin stared at him.

"I was about to do it."

"My intuition is no less than yours, Sir Jeong."

Hyeon Won-chang kicked Jin Yul's head into the stream. The water clouded with something like ink, black and thick, and beneath the dissolving dye, bright red hair showed at the roots.

"Blood Flame Sect," Yeon-shin said.

"You have become a target. They recognised your deeds at Zhongnan." A grin. "As a martial artist, you have truly established yourself. I am incredibly envious."

"What about the women? The ones from the martial arts halls."

"They should be crossing the River Styx."

*This is how grudges in the martial world are formed. Blade Mountain Sword Forest.*

He had truly stepped into the real martial world.

"We should arrive in half a day," Yeon-shin said.

"Let us go quickly."

---

Cheong Myeong sat in the guest room with a cloth wrapped around his head, crossing his left eye.

He smiled. "Yeon-shin. I heard you accumulated unbelievable achievements."

Even with one eye bandaged and his appearance worn, his prominent nose bridge and bright smile were unchanged. A senior who was both grateful and somehow infuriating.

*I thought only Hyeon Won-chang and I would be the ones causing worry.*

Baek Mi-ryeo grabbed Yeon-shin's shoulders. Her hands were ice-cold. Her eyes swept his body, checking for wounds.

"It was reckless. Fortunately nothing serious happened."

The four of them sat in the guest room where they had first met the Mount Hua faction. Yeon-shin asked what had happened.

"The Squad Leader went in pursuit with the Mount Hua Sword Saint," Baek Mi-ryeo said. "The Blood Flame Sect has been confirmed as the force behind Thieving Ghost."

"What about Senior Cheong Myeong's eye?"

"Thieving Ghost used a hidden weapon. The Sichuan Tang Clan's Flying Strike Bomb. Cheong Myeong was dealing with three Blood Swordmasters at the time. He could have dodged completely, but there were civilian houses behind him."

Of course there were.

Cheong Myeong cut in, still smiling. "I also cut off his left wrist. Anyway, we need to raid the Tang Clan sometime --"

The wall exploded.

A detonation of splintered wood and plaster, followed by a voice.

"There is no next for you lot."

Cheong Myeong's hand caught both Yeon-shin and Hyeon Won-chang by the neck and hauled them flat before the words had finished. Flash bombardment pierced through the window -- horizontal, lethal, a sideways rain of energy that would have skewered them where they stood.

Baek Mi-ryeo was already crouching, eyes blazing, seeking a counterattack angle.

But Yeon-shin was faster.

He shook off Cheong Myeong's arm and leaped through the shattered window. The Radiant Wing Sword Style's light blazed along the Desolate Sword as he drew it mid-stride.

Outside: a middle-aged man with a small build and loose silk sleeve cuffs. One sleeve hung empty.

*The Great Violet Pill was not his goal.*

"Blue robe over white clothes," the man said, smiling. "You must be Lightning."

Killing intent spread from him like poison from an opened bottle.

"Are you Thieving Ghost?"

Yeon-shin did not wait for an answer. He walked straight in, drawing his sword, dim waves of light rising from the blade.

An arrival. A threat unresolved.


---
